rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На “Господи взиваю” – 6 стихир: свята – 3 і прп. – 3. Слава, І нині: свята. На стиховні: стихири свята. Тропар прп., Слава, І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. служби свята і прп.
Утреня: по „Бог Господь”: тропар свята (2), Слава: прп., І нині: свята. Катизми чергові і сідальний свята. Псалом 50. Канон свята і прп. По 9-ій – світильний прп. Слава, І нині: світильний свята. Стиховня – вся свята. Тропар прп., Слава, І нині: свята.
Часи: тропар свята, також на 3-му і 9-му ще Слава: прп., кондаки – поперемінно, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – свята, на 3-му і 9-му – прп.
Літургія: антифони повсякденні. Тропар свята, Слава, І нині: кондак свята. Прокімен, алилуя, причасний – свята. Ап. – Еф. 217 зач.; 1, 7-17. Єв. – Мр. 32 зач.; 8, 1-10. Замість „Достойно” – ірмос свята з його приспівом.

troparion_en.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Mother, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Theodora, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion_en.text: You depleted your bodily strength by fasting, vigil, and prayers, / entreating the Creator to grant forgiveness of your sin; / having received it, you showed us the way of repentance, / venerable Mother Theodora.

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Nativity of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Your Nativity, O Virgin, / has proclaimed joy to the whole universe! / The Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God, / has shone from You, O Theotokos! / By annulling the curse, / He bestowed a blessing. / By destroying death, He has granted us eternal Life.

kontakion.text: By Your Nativity, O Most Pure Virgin, / Joachim and Anna are freed from barrenness; / Adam and Eve, from the corruption of death. / And we, your people, freed from the guilt of sin, celebrate and sing to you: / The barren woman gives birth to the Theotokos, the nourisher of our life!

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Mother, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Theodora, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text: You depleted your bodily strength by fasting, vigil, and prayers, / entreating the Creator to grant forgiveness of your sin; / having received it, you showed us the way of repentance, / venerable Mother Theodora.

oca_saints.name: Translation of the Relics of Venerable Sergius and Herman, Wonderworkers of Valaam

troparion.text: Fulfilling the Gospel of Christ and loving him alone,  /  despising earthly cares you settled on an island.  /  There you fought against invisible enemies, /  subjecting your bodies to the Spirit by fasting, vigil and prayer. /  Crowned with glory and now standing before the Trinity, /   O most blessed Fathers Sergius and Herman, / pray that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: You left the world to follow in Christ’s footsteps,  /   reaching Lake Neva1 and the island of Valaam.  /   There you lived the angelic life /   and were translated to the heavenly bridal chamber. /   As you stand with the Angels remember the children whom you have gathered, / that we may joyfully cry to you:  /   Rejoice, blessed Fathers Sergius and Herman!”

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Demetrius, his wife Euanthia, and their son Demetrian, at Skepsis on the Hellespont

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Diodorus, Didymus, and Diomedes, of Laodicea

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Diodorus, Didymus, and Diomedes, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Ia and 9,000 Martyrs with her, of Persia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Saint Euphrosynos the Cook of Alexandria

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “O coeternal Word...”)
With a humble heart O Father Euphrósynos,   / you offered your service as a cook‭, ‬  / and you were truly filled with the Holy Spirit‭, ‬  / therefore the glory of God became known to us through a venerable priest‭;  /  ‬make us partakers of it as well, O God-bearer‭, ‬by your intercessions‭.‬

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You appeared today...”)
You shared in heavenly gladness because of your angelic life‭,  / and you appeared as one equal in honor to the Angels,  / with them, O Euphrósynos, intercede ‬for those who honor you‭.

oca_saints.name: Kazan - Kaplunovka Icon of the Mother of God (1689)

troparion.text: O fervent intercessor, Mother of the Lord Most High,  / you pray to your Son, Christ our God for all, and save all those who have recourse to your powerful protection.  / Defend us, O Lady, Queen, and Sovereign Lady,  / when we who are burdened with many sins, stand before you in misfortune, sorrow, and sickness, / praying to you with tender souls and a contrite heart,  / shedding tears before this, your most pure Icon, and having steadfast hope in you who deliver us from all evils.  / Grant us what is profitable for our souls and save all, O Virgin Theotokos,  / for you are the Divine protection of your God-fearing servants.

kontakion.text: Let us run to the quiet and good refuge,  / and to the speedy helper, ardent and eager to save all. / Let us run to the Virgin’s protection and hasten to repentance; / for the Most Pure Theotokos grants us unfailing mercy as she comes to help all, / delivering her devout and God-fearing servants from great troubles and evils.
